title: War on Democracy Documentary, Hugo Chavez, Cold War, CIA - Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request to share his thoughts on the Oliver Stone documentary 'The War on Democracy,' covering US foreign policy in Latin America, media bias (especially Fox News), the Cold War arms race, CIA interventions in Guatemala and Cuba, Hugo Chavez and Venezuela, and the relationship between capitalism, communism, and Christianity. He argues that US interventions abroad have been driven by corporate greed rather than ideology, and that American media has misinformed the public about socialist leaders like Chavez and Castro.
enrichment.speaker: mixed
topics: Oliver Stone documentary 'The War on Democracy'
US foreign policy in Latin America
Cold War and nuclear arms race
class and race in Central America

Increasingly idiotic and losing relevance; propaganda arm for corporate and right-wing interests
Driven by corporate greed, not ideology; responsible for enormous harm in Latin America
The real problem with communism is rich people not wanting to share; capitalism and communism converge with enough regulation
Christianity is essentially communism; Jesus preached sharing wealth, making capitalist Christianity a contradiction
Has programmed Americans to accept a false view of the world that serves corporate interests
Inadequate, especially in history and civics, leaving citizens uninformed
America consistently backed oppressive elites against popular movements in Latin America for corporate benefit
War driven by personal financial gain through Halliburton; corrupt and immoral
Americans share moral responsibility for government actions done in their name when they remain silent or keep voting for the same people
